children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Service, shall make grants to tribes and tribal organizations for the construction, expansion, or modernization of facilities for the provision of ambulatory care services to eligible Indians (and noneligible persons as provided in subsection (c)(1)(C)). A grant made under this section may cover up to 100 percent of the costs of such construction, expansion, or modernization. For the purposes of this section, the term “construction” includes the replacement of an existing facility.

A grant under paragraph (1) may only be made to a tribe or tribal organization operating an Indian health facility (other than a facility owned or constructed by the Service, including a facility originally owned or constructed by the Service and transferred to a tribe or tribal organization) pursuant to a contract entered into under the Indian Self-Determination Act [ et seq.].25 U.S.C. 5321

children.label: B
children.text: not funded under or ; andsection 1631 of this titlesection 1637 of this title

children.label: i
children.text: have a total capacity appropriate to its projected service population;

children.label: ii
children.text: serve no less than 500 eligible Indians annually; and

children.label: iii
children.text: provide ambulatory care in a service area (specified in the contract entered into under the Indian Self-Determination Act [ et seq.]) with a population of not less than 2,000 eligible Indians.25 U.S.C. 5321

The requirements of clauses (ii) and (iii) of paragraph (1)(C) shall not apply to a tribe or tribal organization applying for a grant under this section whose tribal government offices are located on an island.

children.label: A
children.text: adequate financial support will be available for the provision of services at such facility;

children.label: B
children.text: such facility will be available to eligible Indians without regard to ability to pay or source of payment; and

children.label: C
children.text: such facility will, as feasible without diminishing the quality or quantity of services provided to eligible Indians, serve noneligible persons on a cost basis.

children.label: A
children.text: a need for increased ambulatory care services; and

children.label: B
children.text: insufficient capacity to deliver such services.

children.heading: Transfer of interest to United States upon cessation of facility
children.label: d Transfer of interest to United States upon cessation of facility
children.text: If any facility (or portion thereof) with respect to which funds have been paid under this section, ceases, at any time after completion of the construction, expansion, or modernization carried out with such funds, to be utilized for the purposes of providing ambulatory care services to eligible Indians, all of the right, title, and interest in and to such facility (or portion thereof) shall transfer to the United States.
